---

title: "Homework 6"
output: html_document
---------------------

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)

load("house_prices.rda")
house_prices$year <- year(house_prices$date)
```

# Part 2a: House Price Trends

```{r}
ggplot(house_prices, aes(x = year, y = house_price_index)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~state) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(1980, 2000, 2020),
                     labels = c("80", "00", "20"))
```

# Part 2b: Reshape Data

```{r}
house_reshaped <- house_prices %>%
  gather(key = "measure",
         value = "value",
         -date, -state, -year)

head(house_reshaped)
```

# Part 2c: Combined Plot

```{r}
ggplot(house_reshaped, aes(x = year, y = value, color = measure)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~state)
```

# Comment

This graph is not ideal because house price index and unemployment percentage are on very different scales. Plotting them together makes it difficult to interpret trends clearly.
